title: Write an Nginx Reverse Proxy Config
description:
## Configure a Reverse Proxy

A reverse proxy accepts client requests and forwards them to a backend service.

**Your task:** write a **complete** Nginx configuration file at **`/root/nginx-site.conf`** that:
1. **listens on port 8080**
2. **proxies** all requests to the upstream **`http://127.0.0.1:3000`**
3. passes `nginx -t` (valid configuration)

> 💡 A standalone config needs an `events { }` block and an `http { }` block containing your `server { }`. Test it with `nginx -t -c /root/nginx-site.conf`.

hints.title: Minimal valid reverse-proxy config
hints.body: Wrap a server block in http{} and provide an (empty) events{} block. proxy_pass forwards to the upstream.

hints.title: Validate the syntax
hints.body: nginx -t parses the file and reports whether the configuration test is successful.

validation.description: Checks that the config is valid Nginx, listens on 8080, and proxies to port 3000.
